editorial_v2_applied.capsule_pattern: 4-paragraph stake → size → ack → promise (no 'Answer.cite this' label) — California-payroll-feels-like-a-holiday framing parallels Alabama exemplar

editorial_v2_applied.h2_mix: Statements ('Apply the highest standard per employee, by where they actually work', 'How Teamed runs Idaho wage and hour end to end') + direct questions ('What is Idaho's minimum wage in 2026?', 'When does an Idaho employer have to pay overtime?', 'How does Idaho's tip credit work?', 'Do Idaho employers have to give meal or rest breaks?', 'How often must an Idaho employer pay wages?', 'Is on-call or waiting time paid in Idaho?')
editorial_v2_applied.statutes_in_tables_only: All Idaho Code §, 29 U.S.C. §, 29 CFR § references confined to Source columns in tables; body prose has zero statute references
editorial_v2_applied.no_em_dashes_in_body: Em-dashes confined to inline visual labels (pad-note rule) and pricing hyphen; body prose uses commas/periods/parentheses

high_concern_flags: factual_uncertainty:resolved — Idaho minimum wage confirmed as federal $7.25 per Idaho Code § 44-1502 (state law tracks federal). State preempts local. Page uses $7.25.
factual_uncertainty:resolved — Idaho overtime defers to federal FLSA: 1.5x over 40 hours per workweek, no daily trigger, no double-time, no seventh-day premium. Page uses federal FLSA only.
factual_uncertainty:resolved — Tipped cash wage $2.13/hour with $5.12 tip credit per FLSA § 3(m); Idaho mirrors federal. Page uses $2.13 / $5.12.
factual_uncertainty:resolved — Idaho has no state meal-break or rest-break statute for adults; federal FLSA does not require breaks. Page states 'No' to both.

factual_uncertainty:resolved — Idaho pay frequency floor is at least monthly per Idaho Code § 45-608 (one of few US states allowing monthly cadence). Page uses monthly floor + notes bi-weekly default.

factual_uncertainty:low — Idaho final-pay clock: 10 days from discharge OR next regular payday (whichever first); 48 hours from receipt of written demand for final pay per Idaho Code § 45-606. Verified against Employment Law Handbook + statute text; minor variation in third-party summaries on whether the 48-hour clock excludes weekends/holidays (the statute says it does). Page uses 48 hours excluding weekends/holidays.

factual_uncertainty:low — Idaho HB 485 (2025 session) minimum-wage proposal status confirmed not-enacted as of May 2026 (matches sibling Idaho state tax page flag). Bill could reactivate in 2026 cycle; monitor Idaho Legislature active bills quarterly.

factual_uncertainty:low — Federal FLSA exempt salary basis floor remains $684/week ($35,568/year) following the November 2024 Plano Chamber of Commerce v. DOL vacatur of the 2024 increase to $43,888. Verified per US DOL WHD updates. Further DOL rulemaking is possible; page flags this in body prose.
